content.full_text:
Fern Polrucal PnacrICES CouvttssloN
428 J Street . Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
February 6,2004
Joseph V. Aguirre
333 Aguirre Lane
Placentia, CA92870
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. \Y-04-024
Dear Mr. Aguirre:
This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the provisions of the
Political Reform Act.l The Fair Political Practices Commission provides formal written advice
to anyone whose duties are in question under the Political Reform Act or their authorized
representative. (Government Code Section 83114O).) Under the provisions of regulation 18329
(bX2XA), certain information must be provided:
"(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the
following requirements are met
(A) The nuune, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties
are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an
authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that such
authorization has been made."
Unless a written authorization is forthcoming, your request for advice will be considered
withdrawn.
Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.
L*
Luisa
General
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections I 8 109 --
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
LIse

sections.question_synthetic: The document does not contain a substantive legal question; it is a procedural response regarding missing authorization documentation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a procedural declination/deficiency letter, not a substantive advice letter. No question or conclusion sections present. The letter indicates the request is incomplete and requires authorization documentation to proceed. Labeled as 'incoming_request' because it addresses an incomplete incoming request rather than providing FPPC advice.

embedding.summary: FPPC notifies Joseph V. Aguirre that his request for formal written advice cannot be processed without written authorization, as required by regulation 18329(b)(2)(A), and will be considered withdrawn if authorization is not provided.
